description:
Model a 10 kW residential PV system in Salt Lake City using PySAM.Pvwattsv8 to calculate the percentage of annual energy lost due to a mountainous horizon shading profile compared to a flat-horizon baseline.

System Configuration:
- Weather File: https://energyplus.net/weather-download/north_and_central_america_wmo_region_4/USA/UT/USA_UT_Salt.Lake.City.Intl.AP.725720_TMY3/USA_UT_Salt.Lake.City.Intl.AP.725720_TMY3.epw
- Capacity: 10 kW DC
- Array tilt: 30 degrees
- Array azimuth: 180 degrees
- DC to AC ratio: 1.2
- Module type: Standard (0)
- Array type: Fixed open rack (0)

Horizon Profile (Azimuth/Elevation):
- 0°: 10°
- 45°: 15°
- 90°: 25°
- 135°: 20°
- 180°: 12°
- 225°: 15°
- 270°: 22°
- 315°: 15°

Export the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/horizon_shading_impact.json, including the system_capacity_kw, weather_file path, baseline_annual_energy_kwh, shaded_annual_energy_kwh, and the energy_loss_percent calculated as ((Baseline - Shaded) / Baseline) * 100.
